docs.title: CareRide AI ♿
docs.text: Connecting elderly individuals, persons with disabilities, and patients with verified helpers through a full-stack AI-powered mobility assistance and transportation platform.

docs.title: Problem Statement
docs.text:
Elderly individuals, persons with disabilities, and patients often face challenges in accessing safe, reliable, and accessible transportation. Existing transportation services rarely provide specialized assistance or guarantee that helpers are trained to support users with mobility needs.

CareRide AI addresses this problem by connecting passengers with verified helpers and using AI-powered recommendations to identify the most suitable helper based on:

  • Travel requirements
  • Helper skills
  • Urgency
  • Ratings
  • Availability

docs.title: Project Overview
docs.text:
CareRide AI is a modern, complete full-stack AI application. It intelligently bridges the gap between passengers with specific mobility needs and verified, capable helpers. The React frontend is fully built out and deployed, alongside a Django REST backend, giving the platform an end-to-end working experience for both riders and helpers.

  • React Frontend Completed and deployed on Vercel

  • Django REST Backend Deployed on Render

  • Supabase PostgreSQL Managed relational database

  • Gemini AI Powers helper recommendations and chat assistance

docs.title: Live Deployment
docs.text: Service URL Frontend careride-six.vercel.app Backend careride-ai.onrender.com Swagger API Docs Postman Collection

docs.title: Contributing
docs.text:
We welcome contributions to CareRide AI!

Please refer to the CONTRIBUTING.md file in the root of the repository for detailed instructions on how to submit issues, feature requests, and pull requests.

docs.title: Deployment
docs.text:
• Frontend: Deployed automatically to Vercel on pushes to the main branch.
  • Backend: Deployed automatically to Render.

docs.title: Monitoring
docs.text: • UptimeRobot: Monitors both frontend and backend endpoints for 100% uptime verification.

docs.title: Features
docs.text: CareRide AI provides a comprehensive set of features.

docs.title: For Riders
docs.text:
• Travel Requests: Create detailed travel requests.
  • Disability Verification: Securely upload disability certificates.
  • AI Matching: Receive AI-generated recommendations.

docs.title: For Helpers
docs.text:
• Profile Management: Manage skills and availability.
  • Ride Assignment: Receive tailored ride requests.

docs.title: Universal Features
docs.text:
• Secure Authentication: JWT-based secure authentication.
  • AI Assistant: Built-in AI chat.

docs.title: Roadmap
docs.text:
Future enhancements planned for CareRide AI:

  • Phase 1: Real-time GPS tracking for rides.
  • Phase 2: In-app calling and notifications.
  • Phase 3: Mobile applications (React Native).
  • Phase 4: Multi-language support.

docs.title: Testing & Coverage
docs.text: CareRide AI maintains a high standard of code quality through automated testing.

Run the Django test suite using pytest:

docs.title: API Overview
docs.text: The CareRide REST API is built with Django REST Framework and documented using drf-spectacular.

docs.title: Base URL
docs.text:
• Production: https://careride-ai.onrender.com/api/
  • Local: http://localhost:8000/api/

docs.title: Authentication
docs.text: All protected endpoints require a JWT Bearer token in the Authorization header.

docs.title: Postman Collections
docs.text:
You can also explore the API using our official Postman collection.

View Postman Collection

docs.title: Swagger UI
docs.text:
Interactive API documentation is available via Swagger UI.

  • URL: /api/schema/swagger-ui/
  • Features: Test endpoints directly, view request/response schemas, and explore available tags.

docs.title: AI Recommendation Workflow
docs.text:
The AI engine matches riders with the most suitable helpers.

  1. Request Creation: Rider submits a travel request.
  2. Candidate Selection: Backend filters helpers based on basic availability.
  3. AI Analysis: Gemini evaluates specific needs against skills.
  4. Scoring: AI assigns a match score and generates reasoning.
  5. Recommendation: Top matches are stored in Supabase.

docs.title: System Architecture
docs.text: CareRide AI follows a modern decoupled architecture.

docs.title: Frontend
docs.text:
• Framework: React with Vite
  • Styling: Tailwind CSS
  • Deployment: Vercel

docs.title: Backend
docs.text:
• Framework: Django REST Framework
  • Database: PostgreSQL (via Supabase)
  • Deployment: Render

docs.title: AI Integration
docs.text:
• Engine: Gemini 2.5 Flash
  • Usage: Recommendation Engine and Chat Assistant

docs.title: Environment Variables
docs.text: You need to configure the following environment variables.

docs.title: Backend .env
docs.text:
DEBUG=True
SECRET_KEY=your-secret-key
DATABASE_URL=your-database-url
SUPABASE_URL=your-supabase-url
SUPABASE_KEY=your-supabase-key
GEMINI_API_KEY=your-gemini-api-key

docs.title: Frontend .env
docs.text:
VITE_API_BASE_URL=http://localhost:8000
VITE_SUPABASE_URL=your-supabase-url
VITE_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY=your-supabase-key

docs.title: Installation
docs.text: Follow these steps to set up CareRide AI locally.

docs.title: Prerequisites
docs.text:
• Node.js (v18+)
  • Python (3.10+)
  • PostgreSQL (or Supabase account)

docs.title: Backend Setup
docs.text:
1. Clone the repository
  2. Navigate to backend
  3. Create a virtual environment: python -m venv venv
  4. Activate: .\venv\Scripts\activate
  5. Install dependencies: pip install -r requirements.txt
  6. Run migrations: python manage.py migrate
  7. Start server: python manage.py runserver

docs.title: Frontend Setup
docs.text:
1. Navigate to frontend
  2. Install dependencies: npm install
  3. Start the development server: npm run dev
